conjecture_title: Open problem: removing diameter dependence from Theorem 1.1
summary: The open problem asks whether the log R diameter term in Theorem 1.1 (which requires |K| > 10^4 n log R) can be eliminated, yielding a bound f(n) independent of the diameter of K. No follow-up paper resolving this question was found in the indexed literature. Related subsequent work (arXiv:2406.07718, 'Non-spherical sets versus lines in Euclidean Ramsey theory') cites Conlon–Fox but addresses a different extension (non-spherical sets versus arithmetic progressions) and does not touch the diameter-dependence question. The problem thus appears to remain open.
search_queries: Euclidean Ramsey theory 1-separated set diameter dependence Conlon Fox 1705.02166
Euclidean Ramsey monochromatic line 1-separated diameter independent bound arxiv
notes: Searches returned several related papers in Euclidean Ramsey theory (arXiv:2406.07718, arXiv:2402.12567, arXiv:2512.15516, arXiv:2404.11454) but none address the specific open problem of removing the diameter R dependence from Theorem 1.1. The problem is from 2018 (8 years old), so medium rather than high confidence is assigned for the open status.
